description: Build a .NET-native Redis Vector Library that reaches practical feature parity with redis-vl-java, selectively adopts core redis-vl-python workflows, and is defined by an executable unit and integration test contract.

userStories.title: Publish v1 parity matrix and architecture decisions
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a written parity matrix and core architecture decisions so that implementation has a stable contract against redis-vl-java and selected redis-vl-python workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a parity matrix covering schema, index lifecycle, document lifecycle, query types, caches, and higher-level workflow primitives
Mark each parity item as v1 required, v1 deferred, or out of scope
Document intentional .NET-native API divergences from redis-vl-java and redis-vl-python
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add schema model for index and field definitions
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want strongly typed schema objects so that I can define Redis search indexes in code without writing raw FT schema commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Add schema types for index metadata including name, prefix, and storage type
Add field types for text, tag, numeric, geo, and vector fields
Expose typed options or enums instead of raw string flags for schema configuration
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Validate vector field schema rules
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want vector schema validation so that invalid dimensions, datatypes, and distance settings fail before Redis commands are sent.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate required vector attributes including dimensions, datatype, algorithm, and distance metric
Return clear exceptions for missing or incompatible vector field settings
Add unit tests for valid and invalid vector schema definitions
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Load schema from file format selected for v1
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want to load search schemas from files so that application configuration can mirror upstream RedisVL workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Support loading schema definitions from the file format selected for v1
Map file-based schema definitions into the same strongly typed schema model used by code-first configuration
Add unit tests for successful parsing and validation failures
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement index lifecycle operations
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want to create, inspect, and delete indexes through one abstraction so that Redis search setup and teardown are automated.
acceptanceCriteria: Add API methods for create, exists, info, and drop or delete
Support safe-create and overwrite behavior defined by the public contract
Add integration tests against Redis Stack or RediSearch for create and cleanup flows
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add document load and fetch support for JSON storage
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want to load and fetch JSON-backed documents through the index abstraction so that application objects round-trip cleanly through Redis.
acceptanceCriteria: Support single and batch document load for JSON storage
Support explicit key or id mapping when loading documents
Support fetch by key or id and delete by key or id for JSON-backed records
Add integration tests covering round-trip persistence for representative JSON documents
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add document load and fetch support for hash storage
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want to load and fetch hash-backed documents through the same abstraction so that the library supports both upstream RedisVL storage modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Support single and batch document load for hash storage
Support fetch by key or id and delete by key or id for hash-backed records
Keep public document lifecycle APIs consistent between JSON and hash storage modes
Add integration tests covering round-trip persistence for representative hash documents
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement composable filter expressions
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want composable filter builders so that I can express tag, numeric, text, geo, and logical predicates without raw query strings.
acceptanceCriteria: Add filter builders for tag, numeric, text, and geo expressions
Support logical composition such as and, or, and not
Add unit tests covering generated filter expressions and edge cases
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement vector query abstraction
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want a first-class vector query API so that I can perform nearest-neighbor retrieval with optional metadata filters and field projection.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a vector query abstraction with vector input, target field, top-k, and return field selection
Support metadata pre-filters and configurable score or distance aliases
Add unit tests for generated command structure and edge cases
Add integration tests verifying ranking behavior on deterministic seeded data
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement filter and count query abstractions
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want filter-only and count queries so that I can use the library for metadata search and result counting without vector input.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a filter query abstraction for non-vector retrieval
Add a count query abstraction for matching documents
Add integration tests for tag, numeric, text, and geo filter scenarios
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement hybrid and vector-range query abstractions
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want hybrid and vector-range queries so that I can combine lexical and semantic search and run threshold-based vector retrieval with first-class APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a hybrid query abstraction that combines text and vector search
Add a vector-range query abstraction for radius or threshold-style retrieval
Define deterministic result ordering and score field behavior for both query types
Add integration tests for hybrid and vector-range queries on seeded fixtures
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add typed result mapping and projection support
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want query results to map into predictable .NET shapes so that application code does not need to manually parse raw Redis responses.
acceptanceCriteria: Support raw result access and typed result mapping
Support field projection and alias handling during result materialization
Return clear exceptions for missing or incompatible field mappings
Add unit tests for primitive, nested, and vector field mapping behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add async-first APIs for Redis-backed operations
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want standard task-based async APIs so that the library fits naturally into ASP.NET and worker-service applications.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose async APIs for index lifecycle, document lifecycle, queries, and caches
Define cancellation token behavior for long-running or network-bound operations where practical
Add integration tests covering async index and query flows
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement embeddings cache
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want an exact-match embeddings cache so that repeated deterministic vectorization work can be skipped.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an embeddings cache abstraction with configurable name or namespace and TTL
Support store, lookup, overwrite, and miss behavior without requiring any built-in external provider integration
Add integration tests covering hit, miss, overwrite, and expiration behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement semantic cache with provider-agnostic embedding input
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want a semantic cache that accepts caller-supplied embeddings or a provider-agnostic embedding interface so that semantically similar prompts can reuse prior responses.
acceptanceCriteria: Add semantic cache create, check, and store operations
Support configurable distance threshold and TTL
Accept caller-supplied embeddings or a provider-agnostic embedding interface instead of built-in vendor SDK dependencies
Add integration tests covering semantic match, miss, and threshold boundary behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add selected higher-level workflow primitives
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want selected higher-level workflow primitives such as semantic routing or message history so that I can build AI application workflows on top of Redis without provider lock-in.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement only the workflow primitives selected for v1 in the parity matrix
Ensure each included workflow primitive relies on caller-supplied embeddings or provider-agnostic extension interfaces
Add unit and integration tests for each included workflow primitive
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Set up deterministic Redis-backed test harness and CI execution
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a deterministic unit and integration test harness so that Redis-backed behavior is reproducible locally and in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test fixtures and seeded datasets that make vector and hybrid query assertions deterministic
Add a Redis Stack or RediSearch-capable integration test setup for local and CI execution
Document how unit tests and integration tests are run
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Write getting started guide for core index and document workflows
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want a getting started guide so that I can install the library, connect to Redis, define a schema, create an index, and load and query documents without reading the source.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a getting started guide that covers package installation, Redis prerequisites, connection setup, schema definition, index creation, document load, and a basic query flow
Include command or code snippets that match the public APIs implemented for v1
Document how to run the getting started flow locally against Redis Stack or RediSearch-capable Redis
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add a minimal JSON storage example application
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want a runnable JSON storage example so that I can see the expected setup and document lifecycle APIs in a concrete project.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a minimal runnable example that defines a schema, creates a JSON-backed index, loads sample documents, and fetches or queries them
Place the example in a discoverable examples location and document how to run it
Ensure the example builds against the current library code without manual code edits
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add a vector search example application
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want a runnable vector search example so that I can understand how to define vector fields and execute nearest-neighbor queries with the library.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a runnable example that defines a vector field schema and executes a vector query against deterministic sample data
Show how to provide vector input, select the target field, and inspect returned scores or distances
Document how to seed and run the example locally
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add examples index and documentation navigation
userStories.description: As a .NET developer, I want a clear examples and docs entry point so that I can quickly find the getting started guide and runnable samples for the main library workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Add or update top-level documentation to link to the getting started guide and each runnable example
Summarize what each example demonstrates and the Redis prerequisites needed to run it
Keep docs navigation consistent with the repository structure used for examples and guides
Typecheck passes
